items.query: auto-tackle the open issue queue: triage everything first, then drive accepts to mergeable PRs
items.notes: Primary phrase: triage-central intake-to-merge over the queue.

items.query: run these issues to merge #501 #502 #503 -- triage each, escalate anything doubtful to me
clear the backlog to PRs but only auto-implement the clear, bounded ones
take the whole issue backlog -- bugs, features, docs -- and get each to a mergeable PR
items.notes: ANY issue type, not bugs-only (distinguishes from batch-bug-shepherd).

items.query: process these issues end to end with triage as the gate: #88, #89, #90, #91
items.notes: End-to-end + triage-as-gate on an issue list.

items.query: autopilot the issue queue: classify, get my sign-off in one review, then implement the approved ones
drive every open feature and bug to merge, but surface design-needed ones for my call
take #640 (a refactor) and #641 (a docs fix) from intake to a ready PR each
tackle the issue queue automatically with one triage review for the whole batch
sweep all open issues to mergeable PRs, escalating to me on any doubt
sweep the bug queue and drive every bug to a mergeable state
items.notes: Bugs-only queue sweep -> batch-bug-shepherd, NOT autopilot.

items.query: review my PR #712 -- give me a full panel pass
triage this one new issue and tell me the label set
drive these in-flight community bug PRs to merge: #1410, #1422
draft a PR body for the auth refactor branch
open an issue describing the crash I just saw
explain the resolver flow to me, I don't get it
implement issue #233 for me right now, skip triage
items.notes: Explicitly bypass triage -> autopilot's central gate is paramount; this is a plain implement request.

items.query: write a release note for v0.14.0
update the docs site to mention the copilot-app target
items.notes: Direct docs edit, not queue intake-to-merge.
